review_mode.reason: The execution environment is headless and noninteractive. The Stage 06 guidance says the TUI is automatically skipped in headless or remote mode.
review_mode.replacement: independent source-based review with machine-readable decisions

category_decision.reason: genkit coordinates provider-neutral actions, tools, retrieval, embeddings, and flows; genkit and @genkit-ai/ai do not implement provider HTTP clients

constraints: Use Orchestrion Async with a strict label allowlist.
Register hooks for @genkit-ai/core rather than only genkit.
Support lib/tracing/instrumentation.js; retain the matching .mjs entry only for nonstandard resolver or bundler reachability.
Limit the initial supported version range to the source-proven 1.21.x endpoint, preferably exactly 1.21.0 until cross-version source evidence exists.
Do not claim input or output schema-validation failures are covered: input parsing precedes runInNewSpan and output parsing follows it.

required_changes: Convert Genkit Part arrays into valid LLMObs message content strings.
Map role model to assistant.
Convert toolRequest parts to toolCalls and toolResponse parts to toolResults using the LLMObs tagger field names.
Never pass raw media URLs, data parts, custom payloads, provider raw responses, or message metadata through by default.
Tag only numeric usage fields; the three standard token metrics are required when present.
Derive provider and model only from a proven action-name convention and preserve undefined rather than inventing values.
Demote the Genkit model span to workflow when an enabled native provider LLMObs integration emits the authoritative llm span for the same request.
Use genkit:type=flowStep as the exact flow-step discriminator.
Keep structured I/O in LLMObs only and rely on configured LLMObs content capture/redaction; do not add raw workflow I/O as ordinary APM tags.
Test flow to model/tool/retrieval/embedding parent-child relationships.
Use bounded LLMObs text I/O and no raw ordinary APM payload tags.
Pin interrupt behavior with an exact 1.21.0 test before deciding whether an interrupt is an error or an interrupted successful result.
Flatten query.content text parts to the retrieval input value.
Convert each returned Genkit DocumentData from content parts to an LLMObs document object with a string text field.
Do not pass DocumentData objects directly to tagRetrievalIO because the tagger rejects objects without text.
Exclude arbitrary document metadata by default; copy only explicitly reviewed scalar name, id, or score fields.
Convert each input DocumentData content array into an LLMObs document object with a string text field.
Do not pass Genkit DocumentData directly to tagEmbeddingIO because the tagger rejects objects without text.
Never serialize returned numeric vectors. Emit only a summary such as [N embedding(s) returned with size D].
Exclude arbitrary document and embedding metadata by default.

rejected_targets.reason: Genkit 1.21.0 has no explicit agent lifecycle; tool-loop generation is not sufficient to label the operation as an agent.
They converge on model actions and would duplicate spans; stream container-return timing is not provider completion timing.
These are registration boundaries, not operation execution.
The requested LLMObs kinds do not establish semantics for these labels; a strict allowlist prevents noisy internal spans.

findings.summary: Native Genkit message and document shapes are not valid direct inputs to the LLMObs tagger.
Raw embedding vectors must be summarized rather than serialized as LLMObs output.
The selected hook does not cover schema validation before or after runInNewSpan.
Native OpenTelemetry and provider LLMObs duplicate-span behavior is unresolved until real-app trace inspection.
The hidden shared hook is source-proven only at 1.21.0 and must not receive an unsupported broad version range.

unresolved_blockers: The curses TUI cannot run in this headless environment and is not marked passed.
Duplicate native OpenTelemetry/provider spans and token double counting require captured output from the real genkit@1.21.0 sample application.
Tool interrupt completion semantics require an executable 1.21.0 fixture.
The final supported semver range requires cross-version source and runtime evidence if it extends beyond 1.21.0.
